description_long: This is a single-story residential house with a wide, low-pitched gable roof covered in gray shingles. The front features a full-width covered porch supported by simple columns, providing a sheltered entryway. The exterior walls are finished in a dark material, likely painted siding, with white trim around the windows and doors. A prominent brick chimney is visible on one side, suggesting a fireplace inside. The rear of the house includes a small set of steps leading to a secondary entrance, and the windows are modestly sized and symmetrically placed. The overall design is practical and unadorned, typical of mid-20th-century American ranch-style homes.
description: A single-story ranch-style house with a wide gray shingle roof, full-width front porch, brick chimney, and simple, dark exterior walls with white trim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full-width covered porch with columns and the main entryway.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the brick chimney and a single window, with the roof's slope clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a small set of steps, a secondary entrance, and several windows along the back wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain wall, a single window, and the porch wrapping around the corner.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a full-width covered porch with simple columns and steps leading up to the door.
